llm_result.summary: The case shows broad backlog across life domains (money, chores, friendships, health) with preserved basic functioning and pronounced negative self-evaluation. Modifiable levers should (a) externalize procedural knowledge and routines, (b) scaffold executive functioning and time-management, (c) optimize health and financial self-management, (d) bolster social connectedness, and (e) provide ongoing measurement-feedback to enhance self-efficacy. Interventions combine psycho-social skills training with practical BIO-factors (habits, scheduling, and health tasks) and social supports, aiming to move both domain-specific criteria (C01-C08) and cross-domain functioning while monitoring safety and adaptability.

solutions.predictor_variable: Practical life knowledge toolkit: structured routines + 'how-to' guides for daily tasks
solutions.solution_candidate: Deliver a rapid, personalized life-instruction package with weekly micro-lesson packs and checklists, plus a 30-minute weekly coaching session to tailor routines for morning/evening tasks (e.g., wake-up, budgeting cues, and hygiene/health routines). Create 'how-to' playbooks for money, chores, and everyday decision-making; practice consists of brief behavioral experiments to establish automatic routines.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalizes procedural knowledge to reduce cognitive load, strengthening planning capacity and self-efficacy; practice-based routines directly address perceived knowledge gaps and maladaptive backlog, while normalizing progress to reduce self-criticism.
solutions.measurement: Checklist completion rate; adherence to established routines per week; self-rated knowledge confidence (0-10); frequency of routine execution per day
solutions.safety_notes: If new routines trigger anxiety or overwhelm, slow pace and tailor to current energy; monitor for distress and refer to a clinician if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: How does providing practical life-knowledge tools reduce C01 and C06 backlogs through enhanced planning and routine automaticity?

solutions.predictor_variable: Automated personal-finance management with budget tracking and reminders
solutions.solution_candidate: Set up a budgeting app with automated bill reminders, expense tracking, and a monthly review session with a financial coach or trusted peer; implement a simple savings goal and a debt-snowball plan to reduce financial backlog.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: External scaffolds financial tasks, improving predictability and reducing cognitive load; regular review fosters a sense of control and progress in money management.
solutions.measurement: On-time bill payment rate; budget adherence percentage; minutes spent on weekly money planning; self-reported financial stress (0-10)
solutions.safety_notes: Data privacy; secure data handling; avoid sharing sensitive financial data.
solutions.mapping_query: Will automated budgeting reduce financial backlog (C02) and perceived resource gaps?

solutions.predictor_variable: Household task management system: shared digital board with reminders
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a collaborative Kanban-style task board (digital or analog) with weekly backlog reviews, auto-reminders, and a small daily task commitment (1-2 chores) to prevent backlog accumulation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Visual task management and social accountability decrease backlog and improve planning habits; incremental task commits build routine and reduce constant catching up.
solutions.measurement: Backlog size (open tasks); tasks completed per week; perceived organization (0-10); time spent planning
solutions.safety_notes: None major; adapt for accessibility (color-blind, etc.).
solutions.mapping_query: Does external task management reduce C03 backlog and improve time-management (C06)?

solutions.predictor_variable: Social maintenance scaffold: weekly social calendar + prompts
solutions.solution_candidate: Create a weekly social plan with 1-2 low-stakes interactions, prompts to reach out to friends, and participation in local/community groups; track interactions and perceived connectedness.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured social engagement increases opportunities for relationship maintenance, reducing perceived social lag and loneliness.
solutions.measurement: Number of social interactions per week; perceived connectedness (0-10); satisfaction with social life
solutions.safety_notes: If social anxiety increases, pace adjustments; offer options for low-pressure formats.
solutions.mapping_query: How does scheduled social engagement affect social-maintenance deficit (C04)?

solutions.predictor_variable: Health self-management toolkit: integrated calendar for appointments, exercise, meds, and preventive care
solutions.solution_candidate: Consolidate health behaviors into a single app/calendar with reminders for appointments, exercise, medications, and preventive care; weekly review with a health coach; anchor health tasks to existing daily routines.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces cognitive load of health tasks and supports habit formation; improves time-management around health-related responsibilities.
solutions.measurement: Appointments kept ratio; exercise days per week; medication adherence; health self-efficacy rating
solutions.safety_notes: Data privacy; ensure medical guidance via professionals; escalate if health concerns arise.
solutions.mapping_query: Will integrated health management app reduce health backlog (C05) and improve time-management (C06)?

solutions.predictor_variable: Time-management mastery: weekly planning protocol with time-blocking and cue-based reminders
solutions.solution_candidate: Institute a structured weekly planning session, create time-blocked calendars, employ habit cues to start tasks, and perform a brief weekly reflection to adjust plans.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Directly trains prioritization and planning, reducing chaotic catch-up cycles and increasing predictability in daily life.
solutions.measurement: Plan adherence rate; calendar block utilization; tasks started vs completed; self-rated planning skill (0-10)
solutions.safety_notes: None beyond standard planning effort; adjust for workload.
solutions.mapping_query: Does time-blocking protocol improve time-management deficits (C06) and reduce backlog?

solutions.predictor_variable: Self-compassion and reframing: values-aligned progress journaling
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily brief journaling with self-compassion prompts, values-based progress logs, and cognitive reframing to reduce self-criticism; monthly reflection to consolidate gains.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Promotes self-worth by reframing progress in line with values, reducing harsh self-judgment and promoting resilience.
solutions.measurement: Self-criticism rating; mood tracking; self-worth scale; journaling adherence
solutions.safety_notes: If self-criticism intensifies, integrate CBT-based strategies or clinician support.
solutions.mapping_query: Will self-compassion journaling reduce shame and perceived failure (C07)?

solutions.predictor_variable: Measurement-based care and progress feedback: weekly dashboards and coach check-ins
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement weekly, simple dashboards across domains with a 15-minute coach check-in; adjust the plan based on data; automated prompts guide next steps.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Provides real-time feedback, fosters accountability, and preserves recognition of baseline functioning while guiding cognitive-behavioral adjustments.
solutions.measurement: Weekly functioning rating; dashboard metrics across domains; engagement with feedback; plan adjustments implemented
solutions.safety_notes: Data privacy; ensure informed consent; monitor for distress; escalate if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Does measurement-based care and feedback improve multiple criteria (C08, C01, C06, C07) via enhanced self-monitoring?
